programme.title: Are We There Yet?: The Thief Episode
programme.desc: Lindsey steals sixty dollars from Gigi's wallet and turns back around and blames Kevin for it.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Suicide Possession
programme.desc: Joan and her boyfriend Tony are seeing apparitions in their New Jersey home. Both are concerned that the spirits are influencing their behavior: a non-drinker, Tony has turned to the bottle while Joan has thoughts of harming herself.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Return of the Dead: Glove II
programme.desc: PRS returns to the site of one of their most famous cases, Daisytown, PA, where they had discovered a glove print of an old miner that they determined was the spirit of a Great-Grandfather.
programme.sub-title: Return of the Dead: Glove II

programme.title: Paranormal State: Vendetta
programme.desc: P.R.S. investigates claims of supernatural revenge in the Pennsylvania Poconos. When a client's new boyfriend moves in, does the paranormal move in with him? Or did her ex put a hex on her house and the entire family?

programme.title: Paranormal State: Do Bad Things
programme.desc: After receiving a letter requesting PRS' help, Ryan and the team head to Ohio to help a grieving family and determine if the family's house contributed to the untimely death of a daughter.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Vegas
programme.desc: The PRS travels to Sin City to investigate a 14-year old medium who claims to speak to a dead girl.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Room & Board
programme.desc: The owners of a vacation lodge in the remote town of North Hudson, NY, feel their woodsy getaway is plagued by negative spirits.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Mothman
programme.desc: Ryan takes his team to Point Pleasant, West Virginia to investigate his own personal holy grail of paranormal lore: the mysterious Mothman.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Devil's Hostage
programme.desc: When a woman has long scratch marks mysteriously appear on her back, PRS is called in to see if they are truly facing the devil. But her credibility is called into question, a struggle of another kind emerges for Ryan and the rest of the team.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Ghost On the Tracks
programme.desc: PRS is called to a New Jersey beauty salon, but they soon learn that the building's proximity to deadly train tracks might be the true cause of spirit activity.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Dwelling of the Dead
programme.desc: A single mom claims she is being physically assaulted by a spirit in her Kentucky home. Ryan first explores a natural explanation for the alleged phenomena. But the client's five-year-old son also sees spirits, and his sightings appear to line up.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Lost Souls
programme.desc: PRS and Psychic/medium Michelle Belanger investigate a Pennsylvania home; where a mother named Kathy and her son Michael have both felt the presence of a young boy and hear voices calling, "Mom?" but no one is there.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Haunted Ranch
programme.desc: PRS saddles up to a remote ranch in Hunt, Texas where black shadows are being spotted and animals harmed. What's more, a mysterious crop circle has formed on the property and the horses refuse to cross it.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Asylum
programme.desc: PRS explores a New York State Prison that was once a mental asylum. Ryan and his team go behind bars to uncover the institution's paranormal past and present.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Hostage to Fear
programme.desc: P.R.S. is summoned to help a family who has been haunted for years by spirits lurking in the house. Upon arrival the team finds a family that has become prisoners in their own home.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Messenger
programme.desc: Kelli and her husband Riley are frightened in their coastal Oregon home. They are plagued by a headless apparition and often hear mysterious noises that resemble cries for help from the dark corners of their house.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Desperate Households
programme.desc: When Julie and her family moved into their dream home in suburban New Jersey they quickly realized they were not alone.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Shape Shifter
programme.desc: PRS travels to Shapleigh, Maine where a woman and her daughter are reporting attacks from a winged creature with sharp teeth. Psychic Chip Coffey arrives and senses a half-human, half animal presence.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Spirits of the Slave Dungeon
programme.desc: Ryan books a two-night case in a real haunted jail in Charleston, SC, but PRS soon realizes the spirits themselves are being held there against their will and subjected to an unjust form of torture.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Laws of Attraction
programme.desc: When Paula's young autistic son Michael, with limited verbal skills uttered the words "Go away scary dead boy" -- it sent a chill up her spine.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Bedroom Eyes
programme.desc: PRS travels to rural Pennsylvania to investigate a haunting that began after a new couple moved into an old family farm.?Over the next three days, hidden tragedies are uncovered and latent psychic abilities are revealed.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Cemetery
programme.desc: PRS visits a gothic graveyard, where a jealous spirit seems to be preying on the newlywed wife of the cemetery's caretaker. Ryan traces the haunting to a mysterious, unclaimed cremation urn on the grounds.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Who Is the Lurking Man?
programme.desc: PRS travels to Newcastle, CA to investigate a house with paranormal forces so strong, the family has cleared out. Ryan believes the Native American land may hold the key to the mystery and leads the team in a fight to reclaim a scared family's house.

programme.title: Paranormal State: If I Should Die...
programme.desc: Proven paranormal investigator Ken Arrington leads a new class of characters as they try to help a family cope with what appears to be a demonic spirit in their home.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Paranormal Intervention
programme.desc: Carol Ann stays awake every night taping the voices of the dead. PRS cuts their winter break short to help her curtail her obsession and close this doorway to the dead--for good.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Ladder
programme.desc: A large family in Shrewsbury, MA, has been living with a spirit for the last 40 years. To most of the siblings, the entity is menacing and evil, but Sandy seems to have a different connection.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Man of the House
programme.desc: PRS is called to help a grandmother and her frightened grandson when renovations on her 19th century house stir up the pipe-smoking specter of a former tenant named Alfred.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Death Room
programme.desc: PRS arrives in Ryan's hometown to help the new owner of an old southern plantation unearth its dark history.?A painful family secret reveals a tale of greed and neglect, and the team must help free its unwilling victim from her spiritual prison.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Sickness
programme.desc: Something is making Lisa and her family sick. Is it natural or paranormal? Ryan and the team uncover the tragic history surrounding Lisa's home--with shocking connections to respiratory illness.

programme.title: Paranormal State: I Am Six
programme.desc: Laura has felt oppressed by a violent spirit ever since she heard a chilling EVP in the fall of 2007. The 26-year-old claims she's been beaten, choked, even forcibly submerged.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Room 37
programme.desc: Darrell and Cherry, the owners of an historic Tennessee bed and breakfast built in the late 1800s, are reporting apparitions of a man and hearing whistling throughout the hotel corridors.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Haunted Connection
programme.desc: P.R.S. is called in to help a young mother tormented by an otherworldly connection to a teenage girl murdered in her neighborhood years ago. Could she have psychic ability or is there a more sinister force at work?

programme.title: Paranormal State: Dark Practice
programme.desc: Brenda is terrified by a dark hooded figure spotted in her West Virginia home. The same entity allegedly tormented her late husband. PRS and Psychic/Medium Michelle Belanger help Brenda before she slips into the same abyss as her late husband.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Haunted Homecoming
programme.desc: Ryan brings PRS to his hometown of Sumter, SC to investigate a chilling case where a young boy was tragically shot and killed on a front doorstep while trick-or-treating on Halloween.

programme.title: Paranormal State: First Contact
programme.desc: Washington State, a hotbed of unexplained paranormal activity. Between possible UFOs in the sky and winged creatures in the woods, Ryan and his team are forced to face their fears and deal with the question: Are we truly alone in the universe?

programme.title: Paranormal State: Southern Discomfort At Sweetwater Mansion
programme.desc: PRS heads to the Deep South to investigate a haunted Antebellum Mansion in disrepair. The caretaker and volunteers at the property have many a tale to tell of paranormal activity.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Fury
programme.desc: Kathy, her two daughters and her new husband, "Farmer" thought they had the perfect, quiet life deep in the mountains of West Virginia.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Pet Cemetery
programme.desc: Ryan and his team investigate the bizarre haunting of a woman and her dog in Waterboro, Maine. The Labrador was chewing at her own fur and standing on guard on the bed, growling at unseen figures.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Satan's Soldier
programme.desc: Mark and Kim can't sleep through the racket caused by unseen forces in their central Indiana home. When PRS and Psychic/Medium Michelle Belanger discover what appears to be blood in the basement, they investigate a history of violence in the home.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Codnor Castle Part 2
programme.desc: We stay with the team and find out what happened after we left them last time. The foreboding Codnor Castle Cottage maintains its attack on the Most Haunted team with dramatic effect causing Yvette calls an early end to their night in Hell House.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Croxteth Hall Part 1
programme.desc: The team take on one of the grandest properties in Great Britain, Liverpool’s Croxteth Hall in a non-stop two-hour heart pounding investigation.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Croxteth Hall Part 2
programme.desc: We stay with the team and find out what happened after we left them last time. The team seem to be stalked by something…but what.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Delapre Abbey
programme.desc: The team are joined by five members of the English rugby team as they investigate an all but forgotten piece of British history.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Doncaster Air Museum Pt1
programme.desc: The famous Air Museum in Doncaster houses many an artefact of aviation history and many spirit from its past. Karl investigates one of the huts that is said to be full of paranormal happenings, and he isn’t disappointed.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Doncaster Air Museum Pt2
programme.desc: The main hanger of Doncaster Air Museum is a vast building and houses flying aircraft of every kind. Stuart ventures alone and soon realises something is there with him.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Dorchester Prison 2 P1
programme.desc: Said to be the Most Haunted prison in the UK, HMP Dorchester is a scary place to be with a group of people but alone it becomes terrifying. Karl investigates the upper cells and gets more than he bargained for.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Dorchester Prison 2 P2
programme.desc: Stuart enters the underbelly of this remarkable building and starts to witness some very strange occurrences.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Dorchester Prison Pt1
programme.desc: With paranormal activity of every kind, Dorchester Prison isn’t for the faint hearted. Karl takes on the prison block where aggressive phantoms and poltergeists are said to roam.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Dorchester Prison Pt2
programme.desc: With Karl in the main prison block, Stuart checks out the isolation unit, with dramatic results.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Dorchester Shire Hall P1
programme.desc: Karl investigates the courtroom of one of Dorchester’s most historical buildings and is suddenly surrounded by unexplained occurrences.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Dorchester Shire Hall P2
programme.desc: Under the main building of Dorchester Shire Hall there is a warren of tunnels and cells. Stuart enters this area that is said to house many a phantom.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Drakelow Tunnels
programme.desc: p 8: Drakelow Tunnels - Kidderminster: With over 4 miles of haunted tunnels the team have one night to investigate this labyrinth of horror.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Dudley Castle
programme.desc: The ruins that make up Dudley Castle house a plethora of paranormal activity for Yvette and her team to investigate. Strange noises and a violent poltergeist await them as they turn the lights out and do battle with the unseen forces.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Eden Camp Museum
programme.desc: Once a prison camp during WWII, Eden Camp is now a museum housing many an artefact and many a ghost. Karl enters the cottage to face the spirits that are said to haunt this once family home.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Fort Paull Part 1
programme.desc: Part 1 of this terrifying place concentrates above ground. The team are alarmed at the results from a new piece of equipment and Yvette meets her match.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Fort Paull Part 2
programme.desc: Part 2. The Most Haunted team explore the underground of Fort Paull. With its foundations and footprint dating back 100’s of years the team are faced with some terrifying ordeals.

programme.title: EXCLUSIVE TO PLUTO TV Most Haunted S25: GLOUCESTER PRISON Pt1
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding leads her team in a two-part investigation at the foreboding Gloucester Prison, one of Britain's most haunted places, known for its history of death, fear, pain, suicide, and incarceration

programme.title: EXCLUSIVE TO PLUTO TV Most Haunted S25: GLOUCESTER PRISON Pt2
programme.desc: The second part of Yvette Fielding and the team's investigation of the imposing and abandoned Gloucester Prison.

programme.title: EXCLUSIVE TO PLUTO TV Most Haunted S25: GLOUCESTER PRISON Pt3
programme.desc: The third part of Yvette Fielding and the team's investigation into the very haunted and abandoned Gloucester Prison. With the team splitting up the activity rises and something is trying to stop Yvette and Glen.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Heaven Nose, Mister Smith
programme.desc: Jonathan must stop an angel who's back on Earth with the intention of breaking up his son's marriage.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: The Whole Nine Yards
programme.desc: A chauvinistic coach doesn't want a female wide receiver on his junior-high football team.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Whose Trash Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Mark's friend is running for public office, and he's a very honest man. But things get out of hand when an opponent tries to win by going after his son.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Hello and Farewell - Part 1
programme.desc: Jonathan helps Commander Michaels to deal with flashbacks of Vietnam; he also tries to convince her to go to therapy. Mark deals with his fear of flying.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: The Silent Bell
programme.desc: A pastor must follow a church council's decision to fire most of the staff at the school because they're not Christian.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: The Reunion
programme.desc: Mark attends his high school class reunion and learns that some memories are bittersweet for his old friends.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: The Source
programme.desc: A teenage girl tells school reporters that a driver came on to her right before the bus got into an accident. She may not be telling the truth. Jonathan tries to teach the news writers the correct way to report a story.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven
programme.desc: The problems of the handicapped are addressed when an amputee joins a crusade after a false fire alarm.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Goodbye, Mr. Zelinka
programme.desc: Mr. Zelinka is a beloved teacher who is being forced to retire because he's old. Jonathan and Mark know that he would prefer to continue teaching.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Choices
programme.desc: The parents of two Vietnamese children come to America to look for them after a ten-year separation.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Summer Camp
programme.desc: After having been scarred in a fire, an actress becomes a counselor at a summer camp for blind children. The camp is run by Jonathan and Mark's blind friend Frank.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: The Inner Limits
programme.desc: A young man decides to institutionalize his brother who has been completely paralyzed and speechless for many years, against the wishes of their mother.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: It's a Dog's Life
programme.desc: Mark believes that God has turned Jonathan into a dog. Meanwhile, he must contend with a runaway kid and the kid's scatterbrained mother.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Merry Christmas from Grandpa
programme.desc: On Christmas Eve, Jonathan shows three men what will happen in the future if the Earth continues to be ravaged by pollution. One of the men is an energy official another is a farmer and the third one is the President.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Pilot - Part 1
programme.desc: In the series opener, we meet Jonathan Smith, a probationary angel who has been sent to help people on Earth. He befriends residents at a retirement home. The administrator at the home has a brother named Mark who is suspicious of Jonathan.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Pilot - Part 2
programme.desc: Jonathan helps the residents of Havencrest, when they learn that the retirement home is going to be sold. Jonathan must reveal his real identity to Mark in order to complete the assignment.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: To Touch The Moon
programme.desc: Jonathan helps a terminally ill boy and his mother. Meanwhile, Mark deals with a thief abandoned by his folks.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Return of the Masked Rider
programme.desc: Jonathan and Mark help aging movie heroes to stand up to a street gang known as Satan's Helpers. They persuade a young boxer not to throw a big fight when the gang tries to bully him.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 9
programme.desc: The family are making arrangements to leave Leopard's Den, all except Du Plessis who is steadfastly refusing to accept that he has lost his home. He won't use his share of the money from the sale of the land to buy somewhere else.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 10
programme.desc: The family have been squatting at Leopard's Den as they prepare to make their last stand to prevent the bulldozers from destroying their home. Du Plessis and Fatani bring elephants and a giraffe to dissuade the construction crew from moving in.

programme.title: Wild At Heart
programme.desc: After the devastating loss of Leopard's Den, the family are split across continents. Caroline, Liv and a heavily pregnant Alice are in the UK, while Danny and Du Plessis have remained in South Africa.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 2
programme.desc: Danny returns to South Africa with Caroline, Liv and Charlotte. They are all excited to be back and are looking forward to seeing their new home, Clearwater Farm.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 3
programme.desc: Danny has been preparing his pitch for an Endangered Species Project. JLW, a pharmaceutical company, are looking to fund an environmental project and the family hope that this grant money will enable them to buy Leopard's Den and Mara.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 4
programme.desc: Danny and Ed release two cheetah brothers together, a symbol of their new partnership, but not all of the family are happy about this new start. Caroline is still furious at what she sees as Danny's betrayal of Du Plessis.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 5
programme.desc: The family are still estranged following Caroline's decision to move back to the decrepit Clearwater house with Du Plessis. Rosie is determined to reunite everyone, and plans a civilised dinner as a step towards reconciliation.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 6
programme.desc: Danny is in prison, accused of murdering Peeters, but Du Plessis is determined to prove his friend's innocence by any means he can. Du Plessis' first attempt backfires disastrously when he harangues a police officer and ends up in a cell next to Danny.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 7
programme.desc: With Danny away, and Du Plessis undertaking an ignominious stretch of community service, Ed is left in charge of running the Endangered Species Project.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 8
programme.desc: Danny and Ed are anxiously preparing for a review with JLW, the pharmaceutical company that fund their Endangered Species Project. They are desperate for it to go smoothly, and as a result are driving the rest of the family mad.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 9
programme.desc: Following the loss of their funding, the family are in dire financial straits. Danny and Ed attempt to get an extension on their mortgage but are refused. Fiona spurs Ed on, telling him that the man she used to know would have a plan for survival.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Episode 10
programme.desc: Strange things are happening at Leopard's Den. The family awake to find frogs everywhere, seemingly having rained down overnight; the giraffe refuses to budge from the front gate and the cheetah is off its food.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Scabby Monkey
programme.desc: Contending with overweight cats and their over protective owners was not what Danny Trevanion had in mind when he became a vet. So when a scruffy, barely alive African monkey arrives at his practise Danny is fascinated by the challenge.

programme.title: Wild At Heart
programme.desc: Danny is having second thoughts about the wisdom of uprooting his new family and ploughing all their money into a business venture that they know next to nothing about.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Work To Do
programme.desc: A successful giraffe capture means everyone is in high spirits, until the arrival of Evan and Olivia's real father, Simon, throws the family into turmoil.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Meerly Evan
programme.desc: Evan lets the mischievous meerkats loose in Rosie's room to cause havoc. Rosie is devastated when they destroy an irreplaceable photograph of her mother and rages at Evan. She decides once and for all to go back to Bristol.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Evan Meets Elli
programme.desc: Rogue young male elephants are rampaging through Leopard's Den and destroying everything the family have built, so Danny introduces a matriarch elephant in the hope that she will calm them down.

programme.title: Wild At Heart: Dawn Feeling
programme.desc: At a typically rowdy family breakfast, Rosie is excitedly looking forward to the arrival of her friend Izzy, whilst Danny is concerned about the rumour of ivory poachers in the area. Unnoticed, Miranda the lion cub pads off into the bush.

programme.title: Bridezillas
programme.desc: Binge on the international pop culture phenomenon Bridezillas and satisfy your inner beauty monster. Follow brides on their tumultuous journeys leading up to the big day.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: The Line Goes Dead
programme.desc: When the severed head of a wife and mother is found, Lt. Joe Kenda uses forensics and interrogation to find both her body and her killer.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Snitch
programme.desc: When officers respond to a shooting , they discover the body of a young man they know all to well -- police informant Jimmie Stevenson. it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda to determine who pulled the trigger.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Death Rains Down
programme.desc: Restaurant worker Sharon Coleman is murdered in a hail of large caliber gunfire. Kenda must break through a web of lies to hunt down one of the most dangerous criminal masterminds he’s ever faced.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Blood Sisters
programme.desc: The bullet-ridden bodies of two sisters are found in a suburban house that’s littered with mysterious clues. Joe Kenda learns that the two unassuming young mothers shared an explosive secret.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Metal on Metal
programme.desc: A athlete is gunned down just weeks before starting college, leaving family heartbroken and detectives stumped. The man seems an unlikely target, until Lt. Joe Kenda pries into his personal life.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Run for your Life
programme.desc: A slain body of a marathon runner is found just hours before a big race. With the help of a trusted K-9 officer, Kenda sets off to find the killer himself before he strikes again.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: My Worst Fear
programme.desc: Police are shocked to find a 13-year-old boy killed in an explosion, but their grief turns to alarm when they discover a second, unexploded bomb.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Poker Face
programme.desc: A group of friends discover the corpse of entrepreneur Jamie Foster hidden under a blanket. Kenda e discovers that Foster’s business put him in close quarters with a host of unsavory characters.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: A Prayer for the Dying
programme.desc: A mother of two is found dead in the most expensive part of Colorado Springs, leaving Lt. Joe Kenda stumped—until he looks into her personal life.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Death Grip
programme.desc: When a speeding truck crashes into a residential building, officers find the lifeless body of Michael Faast behind the wheel. But he didn’t die in the wreck – he was shot point-blank in the temple.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Rearview Mirror
programme.desc: When a retired military man runs over a pedestrian, it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda to determine if it was bad brakes or bad intent.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Murder Bites
programme.desc: A young woman is found naked and strangled in a park not 200 yards from her home. It isn’t until Lt. Joe Kenda rethinks the entire case that he realizes someone has been lying.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: John 3:16
programme.desc: A man rushes his friend to the hospital with serious gunshot wounds, reporting they were robbed by strangers. When the facts don’t add up, Lt. Joe Kenda and his team go on the hunt.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Knock Knock
programme.desc: A gathering of friends turns deadly when two gunmen shoot William Davis in the head. Kenda and his team dig into his past and start to suspect someone in William’s circle is lying.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Bring My Baby Home
programme.desc: A four-week-old girl is kidnapped, leaving her mother in anguish and police scrambling to find her. Joe Kenda and Robert Sapp fear she’s been sold on the black market… or worse.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: My First Case
programme.desc: In his first case with the homicide unit, rookie burglary detective Joe Kenda volunteers to work a bloody multi-victim shooting deemed “unsolvable” by the veterans.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: I Will Hunt You Down
programme.desc: When two escaped inmates surface in Colorado Springs, it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda and his Fugitive Unit to bring them to justice.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Raise the Dead
programme.desc: When a tip comes in suggesting foul play on a 71yr old man, Lt. Joe Kenda is left with only one way to reveal the truth: exhume the body from its grave.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Settle the Score
programme.desc: When Johnny Crawford is killed in his easy chair, police are perplexed to learn he may have been shot over a musical instrument. It soon becomes clear the young man wasn’t killed for his own sins.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Peanut & Chico, Leroy Brown, and Tucker
programme.desc: Fighting Chihuahuas learn to get along. A dog with extreme attachment issues learns to relax. A couple’s border collie Tucker is obsessed with moving water, to a point that’s become dangerous.
programme.sub-title: Peanut & Chico, Leroy Brown, and Tucker

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Harley, Annabelle and Memphis
programme.desc: Cesar gives three socially awkward pups some boundaries: a Lab in constant movement, a shrieking Australian Shepherd and Weimaraner with an eating disorder.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Maya, Sara, and Lilly
programme.desc: Maya, an adopted Border Collie/Spring Spaniel Mix loves to jump but is hurting her owner’s neck. Next we meet Sara, an overly-aggressive Shepherd mix and Lilly, a pushy Poodle.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Bella, Rocky and Madison & Catalina
programme.desc: A Chihuahua adds chaos in a previously peaceful home. A boy tries to avoid ankle attacks from his terrier. A five-legged mini Pinscher has found his perfect home, but needs to curb his biting.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Argonaut & Patches
programme.desc: A family is afraid of being bit by their aggressive English bulldog/Lab mix. Cesar prescribes pack therapy. A Cocker Spaniel has a life-threatening appetite for paper products.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Nuna & Kane
programme.desc: NuNu the Chihuahua, fiercely attacks anyone who comes near his owner Tina. NuNu’s total opposite, Kane the Great Dane, is vehemently afraid of shiny surfaces

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: The Dog Whisperer: Cesar Down Under
programme.desc: Cesar’s adventures in the UK continue with unstable dogs and the people who love them. You’ll meet a dog walker, littermates who no longer get along, and a couple who can’t get their dog in the car.
programme.sub-title: The Dog Whisperer: Cesar Down Under

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Chloe and various dogs
programme.desc: Cesar Millan joins the volunteers of United Hope for Animals to rescue stray pets, and to educate the citizens of Tijuana about the serious problem of overpopulation of dogs and cats in Mexico.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Mascot Mayhem
programme.desc: A German shepherd might be evicted because of his barking, and a bulldog with behavior issues isn't welcome at her owner's café. Can Cesar help?

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Coach & Sueki
programme.desc: Coach, an out of control Boxer, bit a neighbor, seemingly sealing his fate. Coach gets a stay of execution when Cesar Millan comes to the rescue. Sueki refuses to walk on the leash.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Buster, Jack & Marley and Sparky
programme.desc: Dog groomer Lauren asks Cesar for assistance with her three worst cases. Jennifer and Michael haven't been able to implement Cesar's techniques with one of their three dogs, head strong pit bull Sparky.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Tillie and Leo
programme.desc: A rehabilitated Wheaten Terrier mix grows overly attached to his rescuer. The owners of a Shar-pei must deal with the result of their dog’s growing aggression towards other dogs.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: A Dog and his Boy; Mobile Vet
programme.desc: Vet Dr. Rick Garcia, needs Cesar’s help with some high maintenance patients. An 11 month old Siberian Husky must learn how to be a member of the family.
programme.sub-title: A Dog and his Boy; Mobile Vet

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Charlotte, Elvis and Jack
programme.desc: Actress Eileen Davidson's Wheaten terrier puppy Charlotte, plays too rough with her son. Two other dogs react to household triggers, the garbage disposal and shiny floors.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Astronaut Dogs & Mongo
programme.desc: Mongo, a pit mix has severe and potentially life-threatening separation anxiety. NASA Astronauts with problem dogs. Cesar blasts off to help knock these canine problems out of orbit.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Hank & Paris
programme.desc: Marsha Alexander’s rescued Mastiff has ruined the furniture and intimidates anyone walking by his gate. Paris, a long hair Chihuahua, looks fashionable, but barks and snaps at strangers.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Most Aggressive Breeds
programme.desc: Any dog can become aggressive, but several breeds seem to have developed bad reputations. Cesar revisits some of his toughest cases and dives into the history of these misunderstood breeds.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Bodhi, Maggie & Molly, and Bojo
programme.desc: Bloodhound/Catahoula mix, Bojo, is aggressive when it comes to mealtime. Molly and Maggie, two territorial Maltese, and a Kerry Blue Terrier in need of some calm assertive energy.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Jersey Shore Dogs
programme.desc: The Jersey Shore is in for a dose of discipline when Cesar heads for the boardwalk to help three families deal with their out of control dog ‘situations.’

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Shadow Dogs
programme.desc: Search and rescue dogs are more play than work and must get their mission back on track. A couple expecting a baby needs help with curbing their misunderstood rescue’s biting.

programme.title: Baywatch: Tower of Power
programme.desc: A new lifeguard helps Mitch and the Baywatch lifeguards confront an upsurge in gang activity, and CJ is enamored with a beach magician who nearly drowns during a bungled underwater stunt.

programme.title: Baywatch: The Child Inside
programme.desc: The Baywatch lifeguards stage a beach-oriented Special Olympics, becoming involved with two young physically-challenged athletes whose parents have abandoned them.

programme.title: Baywatch: Second Time Around
programme.desc: Jesse Majors, the beautiful motorcycle racer that Matt fell in love with, returns, putting strain on Matt and Summer’s relationship.

programme.title: Baywatch: The Red Knights
programme.desc: An ad hoc reunion of elite 1950s lifeguards, the "Red Knights," triggers a bar brawl and Ben's resignation. Meanwhile, Mitch helps deliver a baby when the mother goes into premature labor while swimming at the beach.

programme.title: Baywatch: Coronado Del Soul, Part 1
programme.desc: The Baywatch lifeguards travel to San Diego for a week where Summer is haunted by the hotel's legendary ghost. CJ grows attached to an orphaned dolphin at Sea World. And Mitch is stranded at sea with his brother when their sailboat capsizes.

programme.title: Baywatch: Coronado Del Soul, Part 2
programme.desc: Adrift in the ocean with his semi-conscious brother, Mitch struggles to keep himself and Buzzy alive through the long, cold night. CJ unites an orphaned dolphin with its injured mother, and Summer becomes obsessed with a passionate ghost.

programme.title: Baywatch: Mirror, Mirror
programme.desc: A beautiful woman and her plain sister compete for Mitch's affection with perilous results. Matt confronts the sudden onset of a paralyzing fear of sharks, that may in reality be a far different fear.

programme.title: Baywatch: The Falcon Manifesto
programme.desc: An investigative reporter is working on a story which details the sale of U.S. military technology by businessman Edward Falcon. The story is so incriminating that her life is put at risk. Can the Baywatch team help her get out alive?

programme.title: Baywatch: Western Exposure
programme.desc: Country and Western superstar Jessie Lee Harris takes a detour from his concert schedule and pays a visit to Baywatch in search of his estranged wife and son

programme.title: Baywatch: The Life You Save
programme.desc: When severe cutbacks put the Baywatch lifeguards’ jobs in jeopardy, Matt and Summer volunteer to guard as civilians. But can they convince the county commissioners to increase the budget before lifeguards are laid off?

programme.title: Baywatch: Trading Places
programme.desc: Stephanie Holden spends a week with the Coast Guard to help them crack a dangerous arms smuggling ring.

programme.title: Baywatch: Guys and Dolls
programme.desc: Matt doesn't know whose story to believe when he tries to reunite a runaway teen and her frantic father. Meanwhile, Stephanie must choose between Mitch and Scott.

programme.title: Baywatch: Rescue Bay
programme.desc: A Hollywood producer desperate for a new TV series idea observes a rescue on the Baywatch beach and realizes that a television series based on lifeguarding is perfect, sending Baywatch headquarters into a tizzy.

programme.title: Baywatch: Livin' on the Fault, Part 1
programme.desc: After a minor earthquake, a geological survey team arrives at Baywatch to place an underwater seismometer in the Malibu fault. Hobie has an argument with his father about his future.

programme.title: Baywatch: Livin' on the Fault, Part 2
programme.desc: Stephanie and the geological team are trapped underwater by a massive earthquake! Hobie's and Jackie's lives hang in the balance. It is up to the Baywatch team to save them before it's too late!

programme.title: Baywatch: Aftershock
programme.desc: After a recent earthquake which almost took his life, Hobie leaves California to live with his mother in Ohio. When they both show up at headquarters a few weeks later Mitch is surprised, especially when he hears the reason they are there.

programme.title: Baywatch: Baja Run
programme.desc: John Cort shows up at headquarters and convinces Mitch to compete in the Baja Run dune buggy race with him. What Mitch doesn’t know is Cort is keeping a secret which could land them both in a Mexican prison.

programme.title: Baywatch: Air Buchannon
programme.desc: Mitch has to work a 24-hour shift with Stephanie and entrusts Hobie with the responsibility of taking care of himself during that time. But Hobie becomes infatuated with an older girl and is pressured into throwing a party without Mitch’s knowledge.

programme.title: Baywatch: Shortsighted
programme.desc: Carter McKay has recently moved to Southern California to live with his father after his mother's death. When Hobie invites him to the annual father/son lifeguard competition Hobie learns the reason Carter is so embarrassed by his father.

programme.title: Baywatch: Someone to Baywatch Over Your
programme.desc: When a woman Mitch refers to as “The Mermaid” turns out to be the wife of a crime boss, an FBI agent comes to catch him before he can escape the country. Things become complicated when Mitch and the catty FBI Agent butt heads.

programme.title: Baywatch: K-Gas The Grooveyard of Solid Gold
programme.desc: The beach descends into chaos when DJ Larry "Loomin" Large announces a beach wide treasure hunt for a gold pirate doubloon worth $100,000. The Baywatch team is put on high alert when thousands of reckless treasure hunters storm the beach.
programme.sub-title: K-Gas The Grooveyard of Solid Gold

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: Lance and Cally find themselves the unwitting hosts of a house party that goes completely out of control.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: When the comic predicts that Dizzy will become a rock star, the "Dister" surprises everyone by entering a local "Canadian Idol" type contest.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: When Vern decides to use his expertise in the dark arts to grow his own "familiar", Cally and Lance use the comic clues to find it before the it hatches.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: Succumbing to temptation, Lance joins a group of older kids in a midnight graveyard game only to place himself, Cally and Sage in great danger.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: Lance discovers that Doyle, the owner of Gamerz Cave, belongs to a mysterious secret society: and Cally must face a frightening change in Jack’s personality.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: After being humiliated by Dizzy in front of the entire school Lance exacts his revenge on him in a paintball game that turns ugly.

programme.title: Dark Oracle
programme.desc: When the latest issue of “Dark Oracle” predicts a disastrous outcome for her fashion show Cally attempts to take control of her destiny.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: Error At Daybreak
programme.desc: A shady businessman collapses on an oceanside jetty, the apparent victim of a heart attack, but March discovers blood and a needle near the body.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: Hot Money
programme.desc: After a bank robbery, the suspect, never out of sight, goes to his office where he locks himself in. But when the police search, there is no money!

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Missing Link
programme.desc: A skull, known as the Damascus man, is stolen from a museum and Colonel March's life is imperiled trying to solve the case.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Case of the Misguided Missal
programme.desc: A 15th century prayer volume disappears from a sealed casket inside a locked safe belonging to an Oxford Don; the supernatural is suspected.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Deadly Gift
programme.desc: A barmaid receives a gift, a music box from a thief, dead ten years. But someone is soon murdered and Colonel March believes the music holds the key.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Case of the Lively Ghost
programme.desc: A phony spiritualist believes she's truly summoned a real ghost. Colonel March attends her séance to discover the identity of the spirit's killer.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: Death in the Dressing Room
programme.desc: Murder and blackmail culminate when a call for help during a Javenese dance is the clue that Colonel March needs to prove a faulty alibi.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Blind Justice
programme.desc: When an airline pilot is framed for drug smuggling, a killer comes to his home to silence him... and the pilot's blind wife (Honor Blackman) is the only witness.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Bank Raid
programme.desc: Kidnappers abduct Peter's niece and hold her ransom for $50,000. To get her back safely, Dr. Brady is forced to steal the money from a bank vault.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Strange Partners
programme.desc: A wealthy man uses extortion and a trained attack dog to force Peter to kill his partner and make it look like a heart attack.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Odds Against Death
programme.desc: A gangster forces one of Peter's staff to developed a mathematical system to win at the roulette table.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Picnic With Death
programme.desc: Peter's existence as an invisible man becomes public knowledge, while Sally's friend needs help when her stepfather plots to kill her invalid mother.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Play to Kill
programme.desc: A celebrated actress runs down a hobo by accident and a witness blackmails her, threatening to expose her secret.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: The Rocket
programme.desc: A top-secret rocket is compromised when one of the scientists runs into gambling debts. Peter heads out on a desperate search for the missing rocket.

programme.title: The Ape Man
programme.desc: A crazed scientist accidentally turns himself into a half ape, half human creature, and scrambles to find a cure.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Brainwave
programme.desc: Aboard a United States ship in Japanese waters during World War II, Captain Fielding is critically injured.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: The Death Waltz
programme.desc: At a cavalry outpost in 1860s New Mexico, flirtatious, heartless Lillie takes great delight in playing her two beaus, decent Eustace Fairchild and the sexier, more dashing Henry Buchanan off against each other.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Moment of Hate
programme.desc: A successful but driven businesswoman, Karen Wadsworth, is convinced that she can make her thoughts become reality. She soon begins to believe that she can use the power of suggestion and turn it into the power to kill.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: To Know the End
programme.desc: Emily MacDougall, a young woman living in England during World War II, begins to have strange hallucinations of death and destruction.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: The Trap
programme.desc: A young man discovers a strange parallel universe with dangerous repercussions.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: The Voice
programme.desc: Young reporter Jared Corning travels to a small New England town to cover the trial of several local people accused of setting fire to a barn owned by unpopular Tom Goss. Jared finds it hard to believe that their aim was to kill a pet raccoon.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: The Promise
programme.desc: Following the Second World War German ex-prisoner of war Carl Bremer remains in London, working on bomb disposal units. He marries a local woman and she is about to give birth to their child.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Tonight at 12:17
programme.desc: Laura Perkins is expecting a baby, and the pregnancy is worrying her. She is soon shocked to discover, however, that her condition has resulted in her having extraordinary psychic powers.

programme.title: One Step Beyond: Where Are They?
programme.desc: In 1922 the town of Chico, California was pelted by rocks falling from an empty sky. A writer who called himself The Ghost took credit for the phenomena, but was never identified.

programme.title: Leprechaun
programme.desc: Jennifer Aniston made her film debut in this horror story about a psychotic, six-centuries-old leprechaun on a murder spree throughout North Dakota.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Party Politics
programme.desc: Margaret runs into a distant friend who asks her to have an Ann Summers-type party in her home.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Getaway
programme.desc: Margaret is nominated for the Golden Arch Support award for outstanding service in the field of footcare.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: No Trouble
programme.desc: This charming, witty animated series focuses on the ordinary lives of a middle class dentist and his foot doctor wife. Together, they tackle traumas as being burgled, going on a holiday and deciding whether or not having a child will ruin their lives.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Blood, Sweat and Tears
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret decide they need to get fit.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Holiday
programme.desc: This charming, witty animated series focuses on the ordinary lives of a middle class dentist and his foot doctor wife. Together, they tackle traumas as being burgled, going on a holiday and deciding whether or not having a child will ruin their lives.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Cottage Country
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret get invited up to the cottage of Dr. Yoselheiffer, President of the Canadian Council of Dentists.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Night In
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret find themselves at loose ends one evening when, to their dismay, they find that there is absolutely nothing to watch on TV.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Trouble with Mummy
programme.desc: Bob is concerned when he hears that his mother's best friend Pearl has passed on.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Wedding
programme.desc: Cookie and Melvin invite Bob and Margaret to the wedding of their goddaughter, Tiffany.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Cuckoo in the Nest
programme.desc: When Bobs old friend Clive, (who is a real drunken slob), is dumped by his wife, Bob lets him stay for a few days despite Margaret's objections?

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Gone to Seed
programme.desc: Bob decides to donate his sperm to a local sperm bank.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Driving Bob
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret have put it off for too long. They need to get their Canadian drivers' licenses.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: My Foot Hurts
programme.desc: Margaret gets her own local call-in radio show entitled My Foot Hurts. On the first day, nervous and short on callers, she mentions her favourite curry house on the air?

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Going Dutch
programme.desc: Margaret enters a contest and wins the grand prize: a cycling tour of Holland!

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: For Peter's Sake
programme.desc: Bob's brother Peter is a successful chef who has a cooking show producing in India.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Problems
programme.desc: At her clinic one-day, Margaret sees an old school chum, and invites her and her husband Matt to dinner.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Neighbours
programme.desc: In this story of neighbourhood social angst, Bob is having a complex burglar alarm installed by Graham, a sad, lonely man whose confidence is lifted immeasurably by this rare sale to Bob.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Shopping
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret, concerned at the amount they are spending in take away food and expensive local food shops, decide on an expedition to the Super Save Shopatropolis in Croydon.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Life Saver
programme.desc: Bob inadvertently saves the life of a suicidal man who then decides to become Bob's best friend.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Dental Convention
programme.desc: The time for the annual dental convention has come. Bob is not keen on going when he sees that a colleague from his student days, Bernard Wiggins, a "loud mouthed big shot", is a featured speaker.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Bob Gets Wired
programme.desc: When Margaret forgets to pay for some sprinkles at the local cake shop, she is hauled in as a shoplifter.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Book Club
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret join a book club in the hopes of expanding their tiny social horizon.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Animal Behaviour
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret arrive home from another boring evening out with dreary friends, to find that their dogs behaviour has taken a turn for the worse?

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: The Candidate
programme.desc: Margaret finds herself inadvertently running for City Council when the candidate she is supporting drops out of the race.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Gary
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret thought they knew everything about their next door neighbours, until Joyce and Trevor introduce them to their grown son, Gary.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: New Lease on Life
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret have near-death experience when Bob nearly crashes their car.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Trick or Treat
programme.desc: It's Halloween. It is also Bob father's birthday and his mother wants to celebrate by visiting his grave. Bob is not comfortable with this and tries to get out of it.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Friends for Dinner
programme.desc: Bob and Margaret have been invited to Neil and Moira's for dinner, or so they think.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Tale of Two Dentists
programme.desc: Bob's business is booming, and when Margaret chips a tooth Bob tells Margaret that he is unable to give her an appointment for sometime.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Mummy's Boy
programme.desc: Bobs mother is coming all the way from England to visit him.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Over-Exposed
programme.desc: Margaret accidentally picks up her neighbour's photos at the pharmacy.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Love's Labours Lost
programme.desc: While listening to the radio, Bob observes his charmless young assistant Penny, inspiring him to recite odes about youth and innocence.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Outward Bound
programme.desc: Bob joins his male colleagues on a canoe trip to a provincial park.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Bob's Burglary
programme.desc: While Bob and Margaret are sleeping, the local criminals, Beany and Boney clear their house - TV, video, microwave and electric toothbrush all go.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Patient Dies in Bob's Chair
programme.desc: When a patient dies of natural causes while in Bob's surgery, the local papers pick up the story?
programme.sub-title: A Patient Dies in Bob's Chair

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Jury Duty
programme.desc: Margaret gets called to Jury Duty but Bob has planned a holiday for them and begs her to get herself excused.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: Age Before Beauty
programme.desc: Margaret gets a new hairstyle but Bob doesn't even notice.

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: A Bob or Two
programme.desc: Bob buys an inexpensive painting from the neighbours at a junk sale (mostly motivated by pity since these neighbours have fallen on hard times) and hangs it in his surgery to brighten things up?

programme.title: Bob and Margaret: On Location
programme.desc: A film crew sets up shop on Bob and Margaret's street, giving them a small and unsavory taste of fame and fortune.

programme.title: On the Case: A Killer in Disguise
programme.desc: Police race to track down the unlikely killer responsible for three terrifying murders.

programme.title: On the Case: The Doorbell Rang
programme.desc: The ring of a doorbell overheard during a teenager's final telephone call may be the key to solving her murder.

programme.title: On the Case: Deadly Silence
programme.desc: An 18-year-old woman never returns home after a night out with friends, and the police come up against a wall of silence that keeps them from uncovering the truth.

programme.title: On the Case: Terrifying Calls
programme.desc: A Wilmington, North Carolina, woman is reported missing after she fails to show up for her own birthday celebration. Police never imagined that their investigation would ultimately reveal a gruesome second crime.

programme.title: On the Case: Footsteps in the Dark
programme.desc: A beautiful young woman's is murdered in a dark parking lot. Did she know her killer or was she the victim of a deadly predator?

programme.title: On the Case: Deadly Paper Trail
programme.desc: The police follow a trail of emails, hoping it will lead them to a 38-year-old woman's vicious killer.

programme.title: On the Case: Wedding Day Nightmare
programme.desc: A young woman getting ready to be a bridesmaid at her best friend's wedding is found viciously stabbed to death in her own home. Was the killer also a guest at the celebration?

programme.title: On the Case: Final Words
programme.desc: A devoted mother returns home from work and finds her 17-year-old daughter has been murdered. Was her killer the mysterious boyfriend she had written about in her journal?

programme.title: On the Case: Scattered Clues, Shattered Lives
programme.desc: January 10th 1984. A frigid morning when pilot Ron Holm arrived home from a long flight. As Ron pulled into his Aurora Colorado driveway, he was puzzled to see that the garage door was open and his girlfriend purse was on the ground.

programme.title: On the Case: A Harvest of Grief
programme.desc: Paula Zahn examines the tragic case of a trail of blood and broken cornstalks leads police to the body of a young girl.

programme.title: On the Case: A Test of Patience
programme.desc: The unsolved murder of a teenager torments a small midwestern city for decades until a relentless team of investigators finally finds the clue that breaks the case wide open.

programme.title: On the Case: Circled by Vultures
programme.desc: A tourist takes a photo of vultures in Joshua Tree National Park and inadvertently discovers a gruesome homicide.

programme.title: On the Case: The Answer Was No
programme.desc: Police try to retrace a young mother's last known movements in an attempt to solve her mysterious disappearance.

programme.title: On the Case: Evidence Lost, Justice Found
programme.desc: A young woman riding her bicycle is forced off the road and savagely murdered. Police chase her killer for decades until an overlooked clue ultimately leads to justice.

programme.title: On the Case: The Ultimate Sin
programme.desc: A man's crisis of conscience unravels a decades old mystery behind a young girl's murder.

programme.title: On the Case: A Murder in Coralville
programme.desc: October 21st, 1981 22-year-old Vicki Klotzbach was reported missing. The research lab employee failed to show up for work and no one had seen or heard from Vicki in more than 24 hours

programme.title: On the Case: In Defiance of Evil
programme.desc: A 13-year-old does not return from her daily bike ride, and three weeks later, joggers make a grim discovery. Police connect her case to another girl's abduction and murder five months earlier, but it will take decades to uncover the truth.

programme.title: On the Case: Crossed by Evil
programme.desc: Police race to find a young woman who disappeared on her way to work, but the clues they discover reveal a trail of unspeakable evil.

programme.title: On the Case: Smoke and Ashes
programme.desc: A beautiful bride to be is murdered when a bomb explodes as she enters her home.

programme.title: A Century of Science Fiction: Amazing Inventions
programme.desc: Time machines, disintegrator machines, shrinking rays - they're all here, along with many more of sci-fi's greatest inventions.

programme.title: A Century of Science Fiction: Artificial Intelligence
programme.desc: Take a look back at A.I. history in film and decide whether computers are our friends or they're all H.A.L. from 2001: A Space Odyssey.

programme.title: A Century of Science Fiction: Dinosaurs
programme.desc: Long before Jurassic Park, dinosaurs roamed through a variety of horror films, dating all the way back to The Lost World in 1925.

programme.title: A Century of Science Fiction: End of the World
programme.desc: From Biblical floods to natural disasters and plagues of giant insects, it's not always man who manages to destroy the world, as vividly illustrated in these sci-fi favorites.

programme.title: A Century of Science Fiction: Final Frontier
programme.desc: Science fiction has become almost synonymous with outer space - and with good reason. Take a delightful trip among the stars - at warp speed.

programme.title: A Century of Science Fiction: H.G. Wells
programme.desc: A salute to the brilliant Victorian mind that gave us The Invisible Man, The Time Machine, and War of the Worlds.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Peer Pressure (REDUX)
programme.desc: Dante brings a captive fugitive aboard, along with her teenage son, Ajit. Percy and Ajit's immediate attraction sparks a conflict which endangers everyone.

programme.title: Starhunter Redux: Frozen (REDUX)
programme.desc: Dante must decide whether to save a scientist and his son from the clutches of the Raiders, or use them as a bargaining chip to find his own child.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Midnight Flight
programme.desc: Germania, ca. 400 AD: an artifact called Gunther's Scepter is sealed in a vault. Kleine Burgh, 2001: Sydney and Nigel are hired to find it using a crystal and a stone tablet.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: The Executioner's Mask
programme.desc: When a model's face is disfigured after she puts on the mask of a French executioner, Sydney and Nigel will try and find the tomb to reverse the curse.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: The Royal Ring
programme.desc: When the British Museum gets Sidney and Nigel to help search at Tudor sites for a ring of King Henry VIII, Nigel is confronted with his self-confident pestering elder brother Preston Bailey.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Set in Stone
programme.desc: In a monastery, Knight Gabriel impales a demon, which then turns to stone. In 2001, Nigel and Sydney arrive bearing a letter from a monk, but the abbot claims it is a forgery and shuts them out.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Deadline
programme.desc: Allan comes to Sydney, telling he has 12 hours before a Necron virus kills him unless he delivers Tau to a blackmailer who has the antidote.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Wages of Sydney
programme.desc: Quan Shu, Manchuria 1359 BC: The Chinese invent an explosive gift egg. New York, 2001: A rival beats Sydney and Nigel to a map leading to the Dragon's Egg.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Vampire's Kiss
programme.desc: Lucas Blackmer, a babe-magnet, professor and a novelist asks Sydney to help find Vlad Tepes' lost relic. And he isn't the only one after it.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Mr. Right
programme.desc: Sydney runs into her ex Gray and goes on a holiday with him to Bali, but when Derek Lloyd shows up to enlist her to find Parvati's Bowl, she can't resist.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Sydney at Ten
programme.desc: Sydney's roommate from school is on track of a pendant from her past and enlists Sydney to help find it and catch the killer.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: The Light of Truth
programme.desc: Arabia 843 AD: A sheik uses the Light of Truth to reveal his general as a traitor. 2001: Prince Shareem drops out from Sydney's class to go search for the Light of Truth.

programme.title: Relic Hunter: Treasure Island
programme.desc: Sydney's on the hunt for a Pirate's booty, but will a traitor force her to walk the plank?

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S1 E19 - Nerve
programme.desc: Aeryn has been critically wounded. She needs some tissue samples to heal. The only source is the Secret base the Peacekeeper mentioned.

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S1 E20 - The Hidden Memory
programme.desc: Scorpius attempts to extract the wormhole technology from Crichton's mind, while Aeryn mounts a rescue operation.
programme.sub-title: Farscape: S1 E20 - The Hidden Memory

programme.title: Farscape: Farscape: S1 E21 - Bone To Be Wild
programme.desc: The crew finds a planetoid that's a botanical paradise. Occupied by a scientist and a peaceful girl. But one is a killer.

programme.title: NEW Homicide: Hours To Kill: Do Si Death
programme.desc: Do Si Death: Reality series following US detectives as they reconstruct murder victims' catastrophic final hours to help catch their killers.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Christopher Foster
programme.desc: Failed businessman Christopher Foster killed his wife and daughter before burning their mansion in Shropshire to the ground in August 2008.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Sally Anne Bowman
programme.desc: A look at the murder of 18-year-old Sally Anne Bowman, who was stabbed in Croydon in 2005. With interviews with her family and detectives who solved the case.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: James Bulger
programme.desc: Denise Fergus reflects on the murder of her son. Two-year-old James Bulger was tortured and killed by 10-year-olds Jon Venables and Robert Thompson in 1993.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Beverly Allitt
programme.desc: Beverley Allitt was thought to be a 'model' nurse. Very astute, caring and always willing to help, why did she murder four children and attempt to kill 11 others?

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rolf Harris
programme.desc: Rolf Harris became famous for his artwork and music, even painting a portrait of the Queen for the jubilee. But it all came crumbling down in August 2013 when he was accused of 12 assaults on girls as young as eight years old between 1968 and 1986.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephanie Slater
programme.desc: Estate agent Stephanie Slater reveals the story of her abduction, rape and incarceration, which occurred in 1992 whilst showing a man around a house.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Keith Blakelock
programme.desc: In 1985, PC Keith Blakelock was murdered during a riot on a housing estate in London, triggered after a black woman died of heart failure during a house search. Nobody has been brought to justice for his murder, despite three investigations.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Omagh Bombing
programme.desc: Survivors, victims' relatives and former detectives reflect on the Real IRA car bomb that killed 29 people in Omagh, Northern Ireland in 1998.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rochdale Groomers
programme.desc: Rochdale Groomers: Documentary examining the sex abuse scandal which occurred in the North of England and appeared to go undetected for quite some time.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: The Perfect Murder: Roger& David Cooper
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane examines the case of two brothers who murdered a marketing manager on Christmas Eve 2014 using chloroform.
programme.sub-title: The Perfect Murder: Roger& David Cooper

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: The Hitman And The Hairdresser: Fadi Nasri
programme.desc: A 29-year-old hairdresser was found brutally stabbed on her doorstep in a quiet suburban street of London. A recording of a threatening phone call seemed to suggest the motive, but every lead comes to a dead end.
programme.sub-title: The Hitman And The Hairdresser: Fadi Nasri

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Murder By Mail: Keith Cottingham
programme.desc: In 1984 a parcel bomb was sent to a 53-year-old grandmother living in a tranquil English village. She died of horrific injuries. No one understood why it had happened. The only evidence detectives had was the bomb itself.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: The Double Axe Murderer: Dennis Smalley
programme.desc: A double axe murderer and no clues - except tiny flakes of paint discovered deep in the wounds of one of the victims. This is a story of how forensic science - and the determination of one detective - found the killer.
programme.sub-title: The Double Axe Murderer: Dennis Smalley

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Bodies Of Evidence: Roger Severs
programme.desc: In November 1993, Derek and Eileen Severs disappeared without a word. When police spoke to their son, Roger, he insisted they were on holiday. However, there was no evidence of this apparent trip, and Roger was soon arrested on suspicion of murder.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: The Body In The Suitcase: Lorenzo Simon
programme.desc: On 12th May 2014 a human torso was discovered in a suitcase during routine maintenance work on a Birmingham canal. With no useable DNA and no other body parts, the case initially seemed impossible to crack.
programme.sub-title: The Body In The Suitcase: Lorenzo Simon

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Crossbow Cannibal

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Joanne Dennehy

programme.title: Behind The Palace Walls: Royal Marriages
programme.desc: Any marriages has its' ups and downs but the public scrutiny and media attention on Royal Marriages makes the situation infinitely more difficult.

programme.title: Behind The Palace Walls: Royal Children
programme.desc: Royal Children

programme.title: Behind The Palace Walls: Royal Wives
programme.desc: The life of a Royal Wife has never been easy but the focus of the media in modern times has often made their lives infinitely more complicated.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Revisit: Sheffield
programme.desc: A year after two troublesome searches, Phil heads back to Sheffield to see how a couple of doctors and a firefighter are faring.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Sheffield, Windsor Revisits
programme.desc: Adrenaline junkies Deborah and Justin Beeley had more stuff than they could cope with while Matt Rushworth's entire life could be squeezed into his well travelled backpack. Could Phil and Kirstie find fitting homes for both of them?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Greater London
programme.desc: This week-Kirstie and Phil are in London-with a story of Vicars and Knickers....

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: London, Wiltshire Revisits
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil are back catching up with two couples who had high expectations and long wish lists.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Wiltshire
programme.desc: One couple want room for 4 children and a 4x4 business -whilst the other want a place that will 'give them a big hug' whenever they walk through the door!

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Berkshire Revisits
programme.desc: Our two couples are at a similar stage in their lives, looking to buy their first homes together. Each had set their sights on pricey patches in the costly capital, but did they really know what they wanted and where they wanted it?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Hampshire
programme.desc: They've got a sailor looking for his first home and a soldier who's about to be made homeless.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Maidenhead, Derbyshire Revisits
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil are back to catch up with two sets of house hunters at opposite ends of the age spectrum.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Revisit: Hastings
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil revisit two couples in Hastings, Jakki and Scott, and newlyweds Nicola and Chris, only to find that one couple is now in a very different place.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Kent and Sussex
programme.desc: This week Phil and Kirstie are concentrating their efforts in Kent and Sussex.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Episode 16
programme.desc: With the market constantly shifting, there's no such thing as a "simple search" anymore. Our dynamic duo will have to work harder than ever before to make sure they come up with the goods.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Exeter
programme.desc: The presenters search for properties near Exeter. Kirstie Allsopp helps a couple spend their £600,000 budget on a rural home with plenty of space and an annexe for wife Nancy's mother.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Merseyside
programme.desc: Gary Wakefield and wife Nicky don't try to disguise themselves-they admit they are the fussiest house-hunters in the country.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Warwickshire
programme.desc: It's like Doctor Dolittle meets Lawrence of Arabia as Kirstie and Phil help the camel-racing Fossett clan relocate their ever-expanding menagerie of children and wild beasts.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: South East London
programme.desc: From a design duo looking for their next project to a couple who are finally ready to put down roots and buy a home together this search takes Phil and Kirstie to South East London and on a journey like no other!

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Edinburgh Revisit
programme.desc: A year ago, Kirstie and I were pounding the streets of Edinburgh. I've come back to see if all the hard work was worth it.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: London Southside
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil are searching in South London with three essential workers who need all the help they can get. They're all new to the area, have very modest budgets, and want to find their dream first homes.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: North Surrey
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil take charge of property searches for 2 couples who hope to relocate to the leafy streets of beautiful Surrey –one of the most expensive counties in the country.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Norwich
programme.desc: This week Kirstie and Phil are called to the rescue of faint-hearted first time buyers, Helen and Paul. Scared off by serious-sounding surveys this hapless pair have loved and lost their dream home no less than three times in the last twelve months.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location: Midlothian
programme.desc: Professional rugby player Matt and girlfriend Amy have been giving the property market a try for over a year but so far, have failed to score.

programme.title: Great British Menu: South West Dessert
programme.desc: Today it's the last chance for the chefs from the South West - Henry Herbert, John Hooker and Nathan Outlaw - to prove they've got what it takes to cook at this year's fabulous banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu: South West Judging
programme.desc: It's crunch time on Great British Menu for the chefs from the South West. One chef left the competition yesterday and now only two remain to battle it out, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Starter
programme.desc: This week, the spotlight is on the chefs from Wales – Richard Davies, Aled Williams and James Sommerin. Today they kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks? Former champion Stephen Terry will decide.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Fish
programme.desc: Today the chefs from Wales – Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Aled Williams - pull out all the stops with their fish dishes: poached lobster with confit potatoes, sea bass with brown crab, and sea trout with apple, beetroot and smoked mussels.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Main
programme.desc: Today the chefs from Wales battle it out with their main courses. Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Aled Williams are determined to win, but which dish will take the top spot today?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Dessert
programme.desc: Today is the last chance for the chefs from Wales to impress. Richard Davies, Aled Williams and James Sommerin deliver their desserts, but which dish will earn top marks?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Wales Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from Wales. One chef left the competition yesterday and only two remain as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Starter
programme.desc: This week the spotlight is on the chefs from the North East – Kenny Atkinson, Tim Bilton and Lee Bennett. Today they kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks?

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Fish
programme.desc: Today the chefs from the North East – Kenny Atkinson, Tim Bilton and Lee Bennett pull out all the stops with their fish dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Main
programme.desc: Today the chefs from the North East battle it out with their main courses. Kenny Atkinson, Tim Bilton and Lee Bennett are determined to win but which dish will take the top spot today?

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Desssert
programme.desc: It's the last chance for the chefs from the North East to impress as the one with the lowest total score from across the week will leave the competition. Kenny Atkinson, Tim Bilton and Lee Bennett deliver their desserts, but which dish will earn top marks

programme.title: Great British Menu: North East Judging
programme.desc: Its decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from the North East. One chef left the competition yesterday and only two remain, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Northern Ireland Starter
programme.desc: This week the spotlight is on the chefs from Northern Ireland – Niall McKenna, Derek Creagh and Brian McCann. Today they kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks?

programme.title: Great British Menu: Northern Ireland Judging
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from Northern Ireland. One chef left the competition yesterday and only two remain, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: London and South East Starter
programme.desc: It's the turn of the chefs from London and the South East - Tristan Welch, Tom Kerridge and Anthony Demetre. Today they're out to impress with their starters; breast of lamb with crisp lambs' fry; beer-braised oxtail and ham, egg and chips.

programme.title: Great British Menu: London and South East Fish
programme.desc: Today the chefs from the London and South East region – Tom Kerridge, Tristan Welch and Anthony Demetre want to score top marks with their fish dishes: wild brown trout with onion tart; crayfish Scotch egg; and brown trout, crayfish and watercress pie.

programme.title: Great British Menu: London and South East Main
programme.desc: Today it's the battle of the main courses, as the chefs from London and the South East – Tom Kerridge, Tristan Welch and Anthony Demetre – face off. Which dish will come out on top?

programme.title: Great British Menu: London and South East Dessert
programme.desc: Today it's the last chance for the chefs from London and the South East – Anthony Demetre, Tom Kerridge and Tristan Welch - to prove they've got what it takes. So which dessert will former champion Jason Atherton think is worthy of The Prince?

programme.title: Great British Menu: London and South East Judging
programme.desc: It's judgment day on Great British Menu. One chef left the competition yesterday and now only two remain to battle it out, as they cook their entire menus for the Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Final Starter
programme.desc: It's the finals of Great British Menu. In today's program, the finalists cook their starters, which will be tasted and scored by the Great British Menu judges, and in a twist to the competition, there's a new addition to the judging panel.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Final Fish
programme.desc: Only 8 chefs remain and they are all determined to get at least one of their dishes through to the banquet. Today they cook their fish dishes for the Great British Menu judges, who are joined by a fourth member to assist them in their decision.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Final Main
programme.desc: It's day 3 of the finals of Great British Menu, when the panel of judges give their verdict on the main courses. 8 of the country's top chefs are competing to cook at a banquet, celebrating the very best local produce from around the British Isles.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Final Dessert
programme.desc: It's dessert day in the finals of Great British Menu and the last chance for the competing chefs to impress the judges and secure a place at the final banquet - a celebration of the very best local produce from across the land.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Banquet
programme.desc: The winners have been announced and this special documentary follows the successful chefs in the run-up to the banquet, as they make the painstaking preparations, for what is one of the most important meals they will ever cook.
